title: acer mandshuricum subsp. kansuense
intro: Acer mandshuricum subsp. kansuense, also known as the Chinese paperbark maple, is a subspecies of the Korean maple tree native to the mountainous regions of China and Korea. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which are attributed to its rich content of flavonoids, phenolic acids, and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Acer mandshuricum subsp. kansuense include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and exhibit antimicrobial and antiviral activities. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and extracts, which can be consumed orally to harness its medicinal properties.
